title: Instance-guided radar depth estimation for 3D object detection
container-title: Eighth International Conference on Image Processing and Machine Vision (IPMV 2026)

title: Attention-Depth: An Attention-Guided Lightweight Self-Supervised Monocular Depth Estimation Framework
Real-Time 6DoF Pallet Pose Estimation with Monocular Metric Depth

affiliation.name: Nara Institute of Science and Technology (NAIST), Japan

container-title: Proceedings of the 21st International Conference on Computer Vision Theory and Applications

abstract: <jats:p>Perceiving vehicles and pedestrians in urban environments is fundamental to autonomous driving. Most existing monocular depth estimation methods focus on recovering dense scene geometry, which incurs unnecessary computational overhead and often yields unreliable estimates for small or distant targets in object-level perception scenarios.In this work, we revisit monocular depth estimation from an object-centric perspective and propose CenterDepth, a task-aligned framework that estimates object-level distance by modeling the geometric center of each detected object as a single target depth variable.Instead of predicting pixel-wise depth over the entire image, CenterDepth performs localized depth inference around object centers, reducing redundant computation while better matching downstream perception tasks that require object-level distance cues.To support training and systematic evaluation, we develop a reproducible simulation-based data pipeline and construct virDepth, a large-scale dataset for controlled analysis of object-centric depth estimation in urban driving scenarios, with particular emphasis on long-range targets.In addition, we introduce a local Center FC-CRFs module that aggregates contextual information within object regions toward the center point, improving inference robustness while avoiding the cost of global fully connected CRFs.Experiments on virDepth and multiple public benchmarks demonstrate that CenterDepth improves target center depth estimation across large distance ranges under an object-centric evaluation protocol, while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for real-time deployment.On virDepth, CenterDepth reduces the mean absolute error of target center depth in the 150--200m interval from 13.54m to 3.35m, indicating substantially improved long-range stability.The code and datasets are available at https://github.com/Elodie2001/CenterDepth.</jats:p>
title: Depth as Points: Object-Centric Metric Center Depth Estimation for Autonomous Driving
Attention-Guided Intrinsic Depth Estimation for Unconstrained Consumer Imaging
